content.Text: [Sat 2026-04-18 17:27 PDT] Create a new dashboard page `dashboard/capacity.html` for AgentLens — an "Agent Capacity Planner" that forecasts resource needs. ## What to build A self-contained HTML page (capacity.html) that uses the same styles.css as other dashboard pages. It should include: ### 1. Current Capacity Overview - Cards showing: Active Agents (count), Avg Utilization (%), Peak Hour Load, Available Headroom - Color-coded status (green/yellow/red based on utilization thresholds) ### 2. Usage Trend Chart (Canvas) - Line chart showing agent utilization over past 30 days (simulated data) - Overlay with trend line (linear regression) - Projected future 14 days shown as dashed line - Capacity threshold line at configurable % (default 80%) ### 3. Capacity Forecast Table - Table showing weekly forecasts for next 8 weeks - Columns: Week, Projected Sessions, Projected Agents Needed, Current Capacity, Gap, Status - Status badges: "OK", "Warning", "Critical" based on gap ### 4. Scaling Recommendations Panel - Proactive recommendations based on forecast: - "Scale up by N agents before Week X" if projected to exceed capacity - "Consider scaling down — utilization below 30% for 3+ weeks" - "Redistribute load — Agent X at 95% while Agent Y at 20%" - Each recommendation has severity (info/warning/critical) and an "Acknowledge" button ### 5. What-If Simulator - Sliders for: "Add N agents", "Increase session rate by X%", "Change peak hour" - Updates the forecast chart and table in real-time as sliders change ### 6. Historical Peaks Log - Scrollable list of past capacity events (spikes, outages, near-misses) - Each with timestamp, description, impact, resolution ## Technical requirements: - Use the existing `styles.css` (link rel stylesheet href styles.css) - Same header nav pattern as other pages (copy nav links from index.html header) - All data is simulated/generated in JS — no external APIs - Canvas for charts (no external chart libraries) - Dark/light theme toggle like other pages - Responsive layout - Add navigation link to this page from index.html header: `📊 Capacity` — add it before the alerts button ## Also update: - `dashboard/index.html`: Add the capacity nav link in the header (before the alerts button line) ## Style reference: The CSS uses vars like --bg-primary, --bg-secondary, --text-primary, --accent, --green, --red, --yellow, --purple, --radius, --shadow. Cards use class "metric-card", buttons use "btn". Follow the existing patterns.

Creating `dashboard/capacity.html` and wiring it into the existing dashboard nav. I’m reading the current dashboard pages and `styles.css` first so the new page matches the existing structure and theme behavior.

I have the main dashboard shell and theme helpers. Next I’m pulling a couple of smaller dashboard pages to reuse their section structure and existing card/table patterns instead of inventing new markup.

The existing standalone pages all use inline page-specific CSS on top of `styles.css`, plus the same localStorage theme toggle pattern. I’m checking the dashboard tests now so the new page and nav link line up with whatever is asserted in CI.

There isn’t a root `package.json`; the dashboard tests live as standalone Node tests. I’m checking the test runner wiring next, then I’ll add the page, nav link, and a matching test so the new dashboard is covered the same way as the others.

I’ve got enough context to build this cleanly. I’m going to add a standalone `capacity.html` with its own page-local layout/styles and simulation logic, reuse the shared theme/header tokens from `styles.css`, update the main dashboard header nav, and add a small static test that checks the required sections and simulator hooks.

{"call_id":"call_7ZCbPmM3Ap8NxXDWVL5j3r99","turn_id":"019da322-9750-7132-952c-20de5aabb3ce","command":["C:\Program Files\PowerShell\7\pwsh.exe","-Command","Get-Content -Path .\.github\workflows\ci.yml"],"cwd":"C:\Users\onlin\.openclaw\workspace\temp-builder\agentlens","parsed_cmd":[{"type":"unknown","cmd":"Get-Content -Path .\.github\workflows\ci.yml"}],"source":"agent","stdout":"name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [master, main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [master, main]

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  # ── Node.js Backend ────────────────────────────────────────────────
  backend:
    name: Backend (Node.js ${{ matrix.node-version }})
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        node-version: [18, 20, 22]
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: backend

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Setup Node.js ${{ matrix.node-version }}
        uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}
          cache: npm
          cache-dependency-path: backend/package-lock.json

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run tests
        run: npm test

      - name: Check for vulnerabilities
        run: npm audit --omit=dev --audit-level=high || true

      - name: Verify server starts
        run: |
          timeout 5 node server.js &
          sleep 2
          curl -sf http://localhost:3000/health | grep -q '"status":"ok"'
          echo "✅ Server health check passed"

  # ── Python SDK ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  sdk:
    name: SDK (Python ${{ matrix.python-version }})
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        python-version: ["3.9", "3.11", "3.12", "3.13"]
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: sdk

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Setup Python ${{ matrix.python-version }}
        uses: actions/setup-python@v6
        with:
          python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
          cache: pip
          cache-dependency-path: sdk/pyproject.toml

      - name: Install SDK in dev mode
        run: pip install -e ".[dev]"

      - name: Lint with ruff (if available)
        run: |
          pip install ruff
          ruff check agentlens/ --select=E,F,W --ignore=E501 || true

      - name: Type check with mypy (if available)
        run: |
          pip install mypy
          mypy agentlens/ --ignore-missing-imports --no-error-summary || true

      - name: Run tests
        run: |
          if [ -d "tests" ] || find . -name "test_*.py" -o -name "*_test.py" | grep -q .; then
            pytest -v --tb=short
          else
            echo "⚠️ No tests found — verifying import works"
            python -c "import agentlens; print(f'✅ agentlens {agentlens.__version__} imported successfully')"
          fi

      - name: Build package
        run: |
          pip install build
          python -m build
          echo "✅ Package built successfully"

  # ── Dashboard Lint ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  dashboard:
    name: Dashboard (HTML/JS lint)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with:
          node-version: 22

      - name: Lint JavaScript
        run: |
          npx --yes eslint@latest dashboard/app.js --no-eslintrc \
            --rule '{"no-undef": "off", "no-unused-vars": "warn"}' \
            --env brow...

output.stdout:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [master, main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [master, main]

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  # ── Node.js Backend ────────────────────────────────────────────────
  backend:
    name: Backend (Node.js ${{ matrix.node-version }})
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        node-version: [18, 20, 22]
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: backend

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Setup Node.js ${{ matrix.node-version }}
        uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}
          cache: npm
          cache-dependency-path: backend/package-lock.json

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run tests
        run: npm test

      - name: Check for vulnerabilities
        run: npm audit --omit=dev --audit-level=high || true

      - name: Verify server starts
        run: |
          timeout 5 node server.js &
          sleep 2
          curl -sf http://localhost:3000/health | grep -q '"status":"ok"'
          echo "✅ Server health check passed"

  # ── Python SDK ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  sdk:
    name: SDK (Python ${{ matrix.python-version }})
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        python-version: ["3.9", "3.11", "3.12", "3.13"]
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: sdk

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Setup Python ${{ matrix.python-version }}
        uses: actions/setup-python@v6
        with:
          python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
          cache: pip
          cache-dependency-path: sdk/pyproject.toml

      - name: Install SDK in dev mode
        run: pip install -e ".[dev]"

      - name: Lint with ruff (if available)
        run: |
          pip install ruff
          ruff check agentlens/ --select=E,F,W --ignore=E501 || true

      - name: Type check with mypy (if available)
        run: |
          pip install mypy
          mypy agentlens/ --ignore-missing-imports --no-error-summary || true

      - name: Run tests
        run: |
          if [ -d "tests" ] || find . -name "test_*.py" -o -name "*_test.py" | grep -q .; then
            pytest -v --tb=short
          else
            echo "⚠️ No tests found — verifying import works"
            python -c "import agentlens; print(f'✅ agentlens {agentlens.__version__} imported successfully')"
          fi

      - name: Build package
        run: |
          pip install build
          python -m build
          echo "✅ Package built successfully"

  # ── Dashboard Lint ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  dashboard:
    name: Dashboard (HTML/JS lint)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with:
          node-version: 22

      - name: Lint JavaScript
        run: |
          npx --yes eslint@latest dashboard/app.js --no-eslintrc \
            --rule '{"no-undef": "off", "no-unused-vars": "warn"}' \
            --env browser || true
          echo "✅ Dashboard lint complete"

    branches: [master, main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [master, main]

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  # ── Node.js Backend ────────────────────────────────────────────────
  backend:
    name: Backend (Node.js ${{ matrix.node-version }})
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:

        node-version: [18, 20, 22]
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: backend

    steps:
      - name: Checkout

      - name: Setup Node.js ${{ matrix.node-version }}

        with:

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run tests

  # ── Python SDK ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
